title: Terminal Controls
description: Learn about input behavior, scrollback, copy/paste, and display options

sections.title: Command Input
sections.content:
The command input field is at the bottom of the Play screen. To send a command:

1. Type your command in the input field
2. Press Enter to send it to the MUD server

Your commands are echoed locally so you can see what you're typing before sending.

sections.title: Scrollback Buffer
sections.content:
The terminal maintains a scrollback buffer that stores previous output. This allows you to:

- Scroll up to review earlier game text
- Use the scrollbar on the right side of the terminal
- Press Page Up/Down to scroll through history

The default scrollback buffer stores the last 10,000 lines of output.

sections.title: Copy and Paste
sections.content:
**Using Mouse:**
- Click and drag to select text
- Right-click to copy selected text
- Right-click to paste at cursor position

**Keyboard Shortcuts:**
- Ctrl+C - Copy selected text
- Ctrl+V - Paste from clipboard
- Ctrl+A - Select all text in terminal

Note: Ctrl+C sends an interrupt signal to the MUD when no text is selected.

sections.title: Timestamps
sections.content:
You can enable timestamps to see when messages were received:

1. Go to Connection Settings
2. Toggle 'Show Timestamps'
3. Timestamps appear in HH:MM:SS format at the start of each line

Timestamps are useful for tracking when events occurred during your session.

sections.title: Word Wrap
sections.content:
Word wrap ensures text doesn't extend beyond the terminal width:

- Enabled by default
- Toggle in Connection Settings

When disabled, horizontal scrolling is enabled for viewing wide output like ASCII art.

sections.title: Clearing the Screen
sections.content:
To clear the terminal screen:

1. Type 'clear' or 'cls' in the command input (if supported by the MUD)
2. Or use the 'Clear Screen' button in the toolbar

Note: This clears your local display but doesn't remove the scrollback buffer.
